claim_supported: Press release re-fetched and confirmed this session (dated Dec 11, 2025): TenneT awarded the GE Vernova-Seatrium consortium the BalWin5 HVDC project (full HVDC technology, onshore converter station at Bremen-Werderland, offshore converter platform, 325km combined cable system, 2.2GW rating, commissioning 2032), the fourth award under their 2023 framework agreement. Cross-checked via web search this session against 8+ independent outlets (Seatrium's own investor-relations PDF, Transformer Magazine, reNews, MarineLink, GreentechLead, Baird Maritime, oedigital, Yahoo Finance) all reporting matching facts -- the underlying transaction is real and consistently described, but no TenneT-issued (buyer-side) primary page was found or fetchable (a guessed tennet.eu URL also returned HTTP 403) this session.
locator: GE Vernova press-release body (project scope, rating, framework paragraphs) re-fetched this session; cross-corroboration via web search this session against Seatrium IR PDF and multiple independent trade-press outlets
notes: TIER DOWNGRADE (verifier judgment): the raw record classified this as tier T1 / source_type buyer_procurement / demand_evidence_type buyer_tender, but the fetched document is a company (GE Vernova) press release reporting a contract, not the buyer's (TenneT) own tender/procurement/award document -- per SOURCE_STANDARDS and this wave's explicit tier-discipline instruction, 'company press releases... are T2 at best,' and per the mission-binding demand-evidence rule, vendor-authored reports of their own wins are not treated as primary buyer/tender documents even when the underlying deal is real and independently corroborated. Downgraded: tier T1->T2, source_type buyer_procurement->trade_press, demand_evidence_type buyer_tender->none. Retained as ACCEPTED (T2) rather than rejected because the transaction, scope, and figures are genuine and multiply corroborated -- useful T2 context/geography evidence (DE grid-scale HVDC procurement activity) but must not be counted toward the T1 or demand-primary quotas.
